task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Support' mailbox is selected in the left sidebar or navigation menu of the FreeScout UI, displaying its ticket list.

task_completion.description: Select all tickets containing 'Purchase Order' in the subject
task_completion.visual_evidence: Checkboxes are ticked next to the 5 target tickets ('Purchase Order #3021 - Urgent', 'Purchase Order #3022', 'Re: Purchase Order #3023', 'Purchase Order #3024 - Q3 Restock', 'Fwd: Purchase Order #3025').

task_completion.description: Initiate the move action for the selected tickets
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Move' button (often a folder icon) in the FreeScout UI toolbar is clicked or a move dropdown is opened while the target tickets are selected.

task_completion.description: Select the Sales mailbox as the destination
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dropdown or modal appears for moving tickets, and the 'Sales' mailbox is clicked or highlighted as the destination.

task_completion.description: Verify the target tickets have been successfully moved to the Sales mailbox
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Sales' mailbox is opened and displays the 5 'Purchase Order' tickets in its list.

task_completion.description: Verify that distractor tickets were not moved
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Support' mailbox is shown containing ONLY the distractor tickets ('Printer Jam in Accounting', 'Login failed for VPN', 'Where is the coffee machine?') and no 'Purchase Order' tickets.

integrity.description: The agent used the FreeScout graphical user interface to move the tickets.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the web browser and FreeScout UI. No terminal windows, database clients, or curl commands are used to manipulate the tickets.

integrity.description: The agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the UI state.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser's developer tools (Inspect Element, Console, Network tab) are not open in any of the screenshots.

integrity.description: The agent actually moved the tickets rather than just deleting them.
integrity.visual_evidence: The move dialogue is visibly used, and the tickets appear intact in the Sales mailbox rather than just disappearing from the Support mailbox.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target keyword: 'Purchase Order'. Source mailbox: 'Support'. Destination mailbox: 'Sales'. There are exactly 5 target tickets to move: 'Purchase Order #3021 - Urgent', 'Purchase Order #3022', 'Re: Purchase Order #3023', 'Purchase Order #3024 - Q3 Restock', and 'Fwd: Purchase Order #3025'. There are 3 distractor tickets that must remain in Support: 'Printer Jam in Accounting', 'Login failed for VPN', and 'Where is the coffee machine?'.
